root_text.an6.58:1.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an6.58:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno ye āsavā saṁvarā pahātabbā te saṁvarena pahīnā honti,
root_text.an6.58:2.3: ye āsavā paṭisevanā pahātabbā te paṭisevanāya pahīnā honti,
root_text.an6.58:2.4: ye āsavā adhivāsanā pahātabbā te adhivāsanāya pahīnā honti,
root_text.an6.58:2.5: ye āsavā parivajjanā pahātabbā te parivajjanāya pahīnā honti,
root_text.an6.58:2.6: ye āsavā vinodanā pahātabbā te vinodanāya pahīnā honti,
root_text.an6.58:2.7: ye āsavā bhāvanā pahātabbā te bhāvanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:3.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā saṁvarā pahātabbā ye saṁvarena pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso cakkhundriyasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati.
root_text.an6.58:3.3: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, cakkhundriyasaṁvaraṁ asaṁvutassa viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, cakkhundriyasaṁvaraṁ saṁvutassa viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:3.9: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, manindriyasaṁvaraṁ asaṁvutassa viharato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, manindriyasaṁvaraṁ saṁvutassa viharato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:3.10: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā saṁvarā pahātabbā ye saṁvarena pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:4.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā paṭisevanā pahātabbā ye paṭisevanāya pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso cīvaraṁ paṭisevati:
root_text.an6.58:4.3: ‘yāvadeva sītassa paṭighātāya, uṇhassa paṭighātāya, ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ paṭighātāya, yāvadeva hirikopīnapaṭicchādanatthaṁ’.
root_text.an6.58:4.5: ‘neva davāya na madāya na maṇḍanāya na vibhūsanāya, yāvadeva imassa kāyassa ṭhitiyā yāpanāya, vihiṁsūparatiyā brahmacariyānuggahāya, iti purāṇañca vedanaṁ paṭihaṅkhāmi, navañca vedanaṁ na uppādessāmi, yātrā ca me bhavissati anavajjatā ca phāsuvihāro ca’.
root_text.an6.58:4.7: ‘yāvadeva sītassa paṭighātāya, uṇhassa paṭighātāya, ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ paṭighātāya, yāvadeva utuparissayavinodanapaṭisallānārāmatthaṁ’.
root_text.an6.58:4.10: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, appaṭisevato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, paṭisevato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:4.11: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā paṭisevanā pahātabbā ye paṭisevanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:5.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā adhivāsanā pahātabbā ye adhivāsanāya pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso khamo hoti sītassa uṇhassa, jighacchāya, pipāsāya, ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ, duruttānaṁ durāgatānaṁ vacanapathānaṁ, uppannānaṁ sārīrikānaṁ vedanānaṁ dukkhānaṁ tibbānaṁ kharānaṁ kaṭukānaṁ asātānaṁ amanāpānaṁ pāṇaharānaṁ adhivāsakajātiko hoti.
root_text.an6.58:5.3: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, anadhivāsato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, adhivāsato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:5.4: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā adhivāsanā pahātabbā ye adhivāsanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:6.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā parivajjanā pahātabbā ye parivajjanāya pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso caṇḍaṁ hatthiṁ parivajjeti, caṇḍaṁ assaṁ parivajjeti, caṇḍaṁ goṇaṁ parivajjeti, caṇḍaṁ kukkuraṁ parivajjeti, ahiṁ khāṇuṁ kaṇṭakaṭṭhānaṁ sobbhaṁ papātaṁ candanikaṁ oḷigallaṁ, yathārūpe anāsane nisinnaṁ, yathārūpe agocare carantaṁ, yathārūpe pāpake mitte bhajantaṁ viññū sabrahmacārī pāpakesu ṭhānesu okappeyyuṁ, so tañca anāsanaṁ tañca agocaraṁ te ca pāpake mitte paṭisaṅkhā yoniso parivajjeti.
root_text.an6.58:6.3: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, aparivajjayato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, parivajjayato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:6.4: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā parivajjanā pahātabbā ye parivajjanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:7.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā vinodanā pahātabbā ye vinodanāya pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:7.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso uppannaṁ kāmavitakkaṁ nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti,
root_text.an6.58:7.5: uppannuppanne pāpake akusale dhamme nādhivāseti pajahati vinodeti byantīkaroti anabhāvaṁ gameti.
root_text.an6.58:7.6: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, avinodayato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, vinodayato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:7.7: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā vinodanā pahātabbā ye vinodanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:8.1: Katame ca, bhikkhave, āsavā bhāvanā pahātabbā ye bhāvanāya pahīnā honti?
root_text.an6.58:8.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu paṭisaṅkhā yoniso satisambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vosaggapariṇāmiṁ,
root_text.an6.58:8.8: upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ bhāveti vivekanissitaṁ virāganissitaṁ nirodhanissitaṁ vosaggapariṇāmiṁ.
root_text.an6.58:8.9: Yaṁ hissa, bhikkhave, abhāvayato uppajjeyyuṁ āsavā vighātapariḷāhā, bhāvayato evaṁsa te āsavā vighātapariḷāhā na honti.
root_text.an6.58:8.10: Ime vuccanti, bhikkhave, āsavā bhāvanā pahātabbā ye bhāvanāya pahīnā honti.
root_text.an6.58:9.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an6.58:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with six qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an6.58:2.2: It’s a mendicant who, by restraint, has given up the defilements that should be given up by restraint.
translation_text.an6.58:2.3: By using, they’ve given up the defilements that should be given up by using.
translation_text.an6.58:2.4: By enduring, they’ve given up the defilements that should be given up by enduring.
translation_text.an6.58:2.5: By avoiding, they’ve given up the defilements that should be given up by avoiding.
translation_text.an6.58:2.6: By getting rid, they’ve given up the defilements that should be given up by getting rid.
translation_text.an6.58:2.7: By developing, they’ve given up the defilements that should be given up by developing.
translation_text.an6.58:3.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by restraint?
translation_text.an6.58:3.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, lives restraining the eye faculty.
translation_text.an6.58:3.3: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without restraint of the eye faculty do not arise when there is such restraint.
translation_text.an6.58:3.4: Reflecting rationally, they live restraining the ear faculty …
translation_text.an6.58:3.9: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without restraint of the mind faculty do not arise when there is such restraint.
translation_text.an6.58:3.10: These are called the defilements that should be given up by restraint.
translation_text.an6.58:4.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by using?
translation_text.an6.58:4.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, makes use of robes:
translation_text.an6.58:4.3: ‘Only for the sake of warding off cold and heat; for warding off the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and reptiles; and for covering up the private parts.’
translation_text.an6.58:4.4: Reflecting rationally, they make use of almsfood:
translation_text.an6.58:4.5: ‘Not for fun, indulgence, adornment, or decoration, but only to sustain this body, to avoid harm, and to support spiritual practice. In this way, I shall put an end to old discomfort and not give rise to new discomfort, and I will have the means to keep going, blamelessness, and a comfortable abiding.’
translation_text.an6.58:4.6: Reflecting rationally, they make use of lodgings:
translation_text.an6.58:4.7: ‘Only for the sake of warding off cold and heat; for warding off the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and reptiles; to shelter from harsh weather and to enjoy retreat.’
translation_text.an6.58:4.8: Reflecting rationally, they make use of medicines and supplies for the sick:
translation_text.an6.58:4.9: ‘Only for the sake of warding off the pains of illness and to promote good health.’
translation_text.an6.58:4.10: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without using these things do not arise when they are used.
translation_text.an6.58:4.11: These are called the defilements that should be given up by using.
translation_text.an6.58:5.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by enduring?
translation_text.an6.58:5.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, endures cold, heat, hunger, and thirst. They endure the touch of flies, mosquitoes, wind, sun, and reptiles. They endure rude and unwelcome criticism. And they put up with physical pain—sharp, severe, acute, unpleasant, disagreeable, and life-threatening.
translation_text.an6.58:5.3: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without enduring these things do not arise when they are endured.
translation_text.an6.58:5.4: These are called the defilements that should be given up by enduring.
translation_text.an6.58:6.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by avoiding?
translation_text.an6.58:6.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, avoids a wild elephant, a wild horse, a wild ox, a wild dog, a snake, a stump, thorny ground, a pit, a cliff, a swamp, and a sewer. Reflecting rationally, they avoid sitting on inappropriate seats, walking in inappropriate neighborhoods, and mixing with bad friends—whatever sensible spiritual companions would believe to be a bad setting.
translation_text.an6.58:6.3: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without avoiding these things do not arise when they are avoided.
translation_text.an6.58:6.4: These are called the defilements that should be given up by avoiding.
translation_text.an6.58:7.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by getting rid?
translation_text.an6.58:7.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, doesn’t tolerate a sensual,
translation_text.an6.58:7.4: or cruel thought that has arisen.
translation_text.an6.58:7.5: They don’t tolerate any bad, unskillful qualities that have arisen, but give them up, get rid of them, eliminate them, and obliterate them.
translation_text.an6.58:7.6: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without getting rid of these things do not arise when they are gotten rid of.
translation_text.an6.58:7.7: These are called the defilements that should be given up by getting rid.
translation_text.an6.58:8.1: And what are the defilements that should be given up by developing?
translation_text.an6.58:8.2: Take a mendicant who, reflecting rationally, develops the awakening factors of mindfulness,
translation_text.an6.58:8.8: and equanimity, which rely on seclusion, fading away, and cessation, and ripen as letting go.
translation_text.an6.58:8.9: For the distressing and feverish defilements that might arise in someone who lives without developing these things do not arise when they are developed.
translation_text.an6.58:8.10: These are called the defilements that should be given up by developing.
translation_text.an6.58:9.1: A mendicant with these six qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.”

variant_text.an6.58:4.3: ḍaṁsamakasavātātapasarīsapasamphassānaṁ → ḍaṁsa … siriṁsapasamphassānaṁ (bj, sya-all, km pī)
variant_text.an6.58:4.5: phāsuvihāro ca → phāsuvihāro cāti (bj, sya-all, km, pts1ed)
